video_title: The Stock Market Is Moving Onchain
gist: Ondo Finance's Ian Debode explains how the firm minted a tokenized SpaceX stock on Ethereum within ~5 minutes of its June 12 NASDAQ IPO using pre-integrated RFQ market makers instead of DEX pools, and lays out the thesis that letting market makers post tokenized stocks as collateral on Ondo's new offshore perp platform solves the capital inefficiency that keeps single-name equity perp liquidity thin on Hyperliquid and Trade XYZ. He frames NYSE/NASDAQ private-blockchain tokenization as synergistic rather than competitive, argues perps are an offshore-favored instrument that could grow to the size of the options market, and targets $1.5-2B in tokenized stocks by end of 2026.

insights.claim: Ondo Finance minted a tokenized SpaceX stock on Ethereum roughly 5 minutes after SpaceX began trading on NASDAQ on June 12, by pre-deploying the token and pre-integrating market makers before the shares were tradable.
insights.speaker: Ian Debode, Ondo
insights.so_what: Speed-to-tokenize a newly public equity is a competitive moat; watch which issuer is live first on future high-profile IPOs.
insights.quote: I think we were live with trading about 5 minutes after the real thing started trading.

insights.claim: Buyers who bought Ondo's tokenized SpaceX at IPO are up roughly 30% since June 12.
insights.speaker: Ian Debode, Ondo
insights.so_what: Signals strong post-IPO demand for tokenized SpaceX exposure among offshore crypto buyers.
insights.quote: those who did quite frankly I think are about up 30% or so.

insights.claim: Ondo deliberately avoided pre-IPO allocation for SpaceX because IPO allocations go to large institutions and over-promising them creates disappointment, citing that xStocks failed to set expectations correctly and left Bybit and Binance Wallet disappointed.
insights.speaker: Ian Debode, Ondo
insights.so_what: Pre-IPO tokenization carries allocation-failure risk; Ondo's model of waiting for the exchange listing avoids under-delivery to distribution partners.
insights.quote: particularly with Xtocks quite frankly who I think uh did not set expectations in the right way.

insights.claim: Ondo is considering offering pre-IPO tokenized stocks for private companies including OpenAI and Anthropic.
insights.speaker: Ian Debode, Ondo
insights.so_what: Pre-IPO tokenized exposure to marquee AI names is a likely next product battleground among RWA issuers.

insights.claim: Ondo's tokenized stocks trade via just-in-time RFQ minting with no DEX pools: on a buy click, onboarded market makers are pinged, Ondo quotes the live NYSE/NASDAQ price plus 5 basis points as a volatility buffer, and the best-execution market maker mints tokens against the buyer's stablecoins.
insights.speaker: Ian Debode, Ondo
insights.so_what: The RFQ-plus-5bps model taps existing TradFi liquidity directly, giving tighter execution than AMM pools and defining the winning market structure for tokenized equities.
insights.quote: We check exactly what is the price available on the New York Stock Exchange or the NASDAQ and we add five bips.

insights.claim: After minting a tokenized stock, Ondo buys the underlying share on the open market within a few hundred milliseconds to fully collateralize the token, with the 5bps charge covering intra-window price moves.
insights.speaker: Ian Debode, Ondo
insights.so_what: Sub-second hedging keeps Ondo's price-volatility exposure inside its 5bps padding, so the tokens stay fully backed 1:1.

insights.claim: End-to-end roundtrip for a tokenized-stock purchase is about 1 second on the BNB Chain or Solana and up to about 12 seconds (three blocks via Cow Swap) on Ethereum.
insights.speaker: Ian Debode, Ondo
insights.so_what: Chain choice materially affects settlement latency for tokenized equity trades.
insights.quote: Uh on the Binance chain or Salana about a second.

insights.claim: Around 80-90% of Ondo's tokenized-stock flow originates outside its own platform through integrated wallets and exchanges like Binance, Bitget, Gate, OKX, MetaMask and Phantom, yet still routes through Ondo's RFQ market makers.
insights.speaker: Ian Debode, Ondo
insights.so_what: Ondo's growth is driven by wallet/exchange distribution partners with larger user bases, not its own front end.
insights.quote: 80% is outside of our platform. 20% in the platform.

insights.claim: DEX-pool liquidity for tokenized stocks is misleading: a $1M order against a competitor's $6M Solana DEX pool would incur ~10% slippage versus ~0.1% through Ondo's RFQ routing.
insights.speaker: Ian Debode, Ondo
insights.so_what: Advertised DEX-pool TVL is not a real liquidity signal for tokenized equities; discount competitors that quote pool depth.
insights.quote: It would be 10% slippage on a DEX pool and.1% slippage vio.

insights.claim: AMM pools remain necessary specifically for weekend trading of tokenized stocks, while just-in-time RFQ liquidity is used during 24/5 market hours and overnight.
insights.speaker: Ian Debode, Ondo
insights.so_what: Weekend price discovery for tokenized stocks still depends on AMMs until 24/7 underlying markets exist.

insights.claim: Circle is Ondo's single most-bought tokenized stock by a wide margin, reportedly driven by Chinese trader groups who view it as 'the next Nvidia.'
insights.speaker: Ian Debode, Ondo
insights.so_what: Tokenized-stock flow skews toward speculative retail narratives (Circle over blue chips), a signal of who the offshore buyer base actually is.
insights.quote: Circle is surprisingly so the number one uh by a mile actually.

insights.claim: After Circle, Ondo's most popular tokenized stocks are Micron and other semiconductor names, plus Nvidia, Tesla and the Magnificent 7, with SpaceX climbing rapidly.
insights.speaker: Ian Debode, Ondo
insights.so_what: Semis and mega-cap tech dominate tokenized-equity demand, guiding which underlyings issuers prioritize.

insights.claim: DeFi lending markets for tokenized stocks are only now emerging on Morpho and Kamino because DeFi was architected around ~20 crypto assets, not the hundreds of $50B+ market-cap equities that tokenization introduces.
insights.speaker: Ian Debode, Ondo
insights.so_what: The DeFi collateral/lending stack for equities is an early build-out; watch Morpho and Kamino vaults as the integration surface expands.

insights.claim: Single-name equity perps have poor liquidity because market makers taking the short side must hedge by buying the actual stock in an offchain brokerage, stranding collateral offchain and forcing them to post extra onchain stablecoins, yielding at best ~50% capital efficiency.
insights.speaker: Ian Debode, Ondo
insights.so_what: Explains why single-name equity perp order books are thin today and identifies the exact constraint any competitor must solve.
insights.quote: just not very capital efficient at best probably 50%

insights.claim: Ondo's perp platform lets market makers post tokenized stocks (e.g. tokenized Tesla) as collateral directly on the venue, making their short fully hedged in one place and pushing capital efficiency toward ~100%, which deepens liquidity and triggers a trader-attraction flywheel.
insights.speaker: Ian Debode, Ondo
insights.so_what: Same-venue tokenized-stock collateral is the mechanism Ondo bets will win single-name equity perp liquidity.

insights.claim: Ondo argues only the token issuer can safely accept tokenized stocks as perp collateral, because it sits on the actual backing and can liquidate positions in size directly through NYSE/NASDAQ rails, which is why rival perp venues cannot replicate the model.
insights.speaker: Ian Debode, Ondo
insights.so_what: Vertical integration of issuance plus perp venue is Ondo's structural moat against Hyperliquid and other perp DEXs.
insights.quote: We sit on the backing of the stocks, not hyper liquid.

insights.claim: Adding tokenized underlyings as perp collateral unlocks pair trades (e.g. long gold, short silver), retail portfolio margin approaching institutional prime brokerage, and structured products like a Mag7 ETF wrapper that runs a weekend carry trade collecting perp funding.
insights.speaker: Ian Debode, Ondo
insights.so_what: Points to new onchain product categories (portfolio margin, carry-enhanced ETFs) that could be productized for TradFi brokerages.

insights.claim: Ian Debode predicts the next 12-18 months will see dramatic innovation in onchain asset and wealth management as tokenized stocks, ETFs, prediction markets and commodities platforms become composable building blocks.
insights.speaker: Ian Debode, Ondo
insights.so_what: Frames a 12-18 month window in which onchain asset-management products go from primitives to distributed strategies.
insights.quote: people are going to dramatically innovate in how asset management and even wealth management is done

themes.theme: The perp thesis: offshore vs onshore

insights.claim: On a 10-year horizon Ian Debode sees no reason the perpetual futures market cannot grow to the same size as the options market, positioning perps as a mature standalone product category alongside options.
insights.speaker: Ian Debode, Ondo
insights.so_what: Bull case sizing perps against the options market implies a large TAM for perp-focused venues and infrastructure.
insights.quote: I don't see why a perp wouldn't be able to grow into a same size as like the options market.

insights.claim: The perp is fundamentally an offshore-favored instrument because US retail leverage caps are far lower than offshore, so separate onshore (regulated, lower leverage) and offshore (higher leverage) markets will coexist and remain very different.
insights.speaker: Ian Debode, Ondo
insights.so_what: Investors should expect bifurcated perp liquidity, with the more expressive markets remaining offshore.

insights.claim: Ian Debode rejects the maximalist view that spot markets become unnecessary once everything is perped, arguing a world of only funding-rate-driven derivatives would be 'financial crisis 2.0.'
insights.speaker: Ian Debode, Ondo
insights.so_what: A prominent RWA builder pushes back on perp-maximalism, arguing spot must underpin the derivative layer.

insights.claim: Ian Debode warns US regulators that overly restrictive rules will leave the US unable to compete with a fast-growing offshore tokenized-stock and perp market, noting the offshore 'wrapper' concept has no legal equivalent in the US.
insights.speaker: Ian Debode, Ondo
insights.so_what: Regulatory arbitrage favors offshore issuers today; US framework changes on wrappers/third-party custodial issuance are the key catalyst to watch.

insights.claim: Ondo's perp platform executes entirely offchain in a secure enclave for speed and privacy, with onchain multi-chain deposits/withdrawals and a decentralized attester model that publishes the execution code so Ondo cannot unilaterally change it or rug users, making it non-custodial.
insights.speaker: Ian Debode, Ondo
insights.so_what: A hybrid CEX/DEX design that trades full onchain composability for performance and privacy while preserving self-custody.

insights.claim: Ondo built its secure-enclave perp technology by acquiring Enclave Markets earlier this year.
insights.speaker: Ian Debode, Ondo
insights.so_what: Identifies the specific acquired technology stack underpinning Ondo's perp venue.

insights.claim: Ondo chose to build its own perp stack rather than deploy on Hyperliquid via HIP-3 because Hyperliquid makes every position fully public, runs at ~600ms latency, and supports only HYPE, Bitcoin and stables as collateral rather than tokenized stocks.
insights.speaker: Ian Debode, Ondo
insights.so_what: Owning the margin engine is required to list tokenized stocks as collateral, explaining why Ondo did not build atop Hyperliquid.
insights.quote: even on hyperlquid, you're looking at 600 milliseconds.

insights.claim: Ian Debode is not worried about NYSE and NASDAQ building their own private tokenization blockchains, arguing their move toward 24/7 markets directly benefits Ondo.
insights.speaker: Ian Debode, Ondo
insights.so_what: Reframes incumbent tokenization as infrastructure Ondo consumes rather than a competitive threat to crypto-native issuers.
insights.quote: the more New York Stock Exchange and NASDAQ can move towards 24/7 markets, the better for us

insights.claim: Ondo can currently only mint and redeem tokenized stocks 24/5 because NYSE and NASDAQ close on weekends, so exchange weekend trading would let DeFi liquidations and redemptions run 24/7 with Ondo as a major client buying and selling the underlying.
insights.speaker: Ian Debode, Ondo
insights.so_what: The 24/5 vs 24/7 mismatch is the key operational bottleneck for onchain stocks; incumbent weekend markets are the unlock.

insights.claim: Ondo targets $1.5-2B in tokenized stocks by end of 2026, alongside shipping its perp platform to general availability with deep liquidity and launching third-party 'strategies'/portfolio products for asset managers.
insights.speaker: Ian Debode, Ondo
insights.so_what: Concrete AUM and product milestones to benchmark Ondo's execution against through year-end 2026.
insights.quote: call it billion and a half, ideally two billion in tokenized stocks.

insights.claim: Ondo's perp platform launched a public beta the prior week with general availability expected within a couple of weeks, and demand has exceeded internal expectations.
insights.speaker: Ian Debode, Ondo
insights.so_what: Near-term launch timeline and stronger-than-expected demand signal traction for Ondo's equity perp venue.

insights.claim: Ondo co-founder Nathan Allman passed away, and Ian Debode says the firm's product roadmap remains crystal clear because Allman had already laid out the full vision for tokenized stocks and perps.
insights.speaker: Ian Debode, Ondo
insights.so_what: Key-person loss at a leading RWA issuer, though management signals continuity of the existing roadmap.
